entity:SsoUsedToken.summary: SsoUsedToken table — replay protection for SSO handoff JWTs (jti uniqueness)

entity:TicketParticipant.summary: Live CC participant list for a ticket; reconciled on each agent reply, auto-populated from inbound third-party senders

entity:GithubIssueEvent.summary: GithubIssueEvent table — append-only activity timeline for a linked GitHub issue

route:POST /api/v1/tickets/:id/github/ac.summary: POST /api/v1/tickets/:id/github/acknowledge → TicketsController.acknowledgeGithub (clears the GitHub attention flag)

route:POST /api/v1/auth/sso.summary: POST /api/v1/auth/sso → AuthController.ssoAuth — exchanges a host-signed HS256 handoff JWT for a portal session

route:GET /api/v1/config/field-usage.summary: GET /api/v1/config/field-usage → ConfigController.fieldUsage (admin) — per-value ticket counts for field1/field2, powers the delete-option warning

ext:MinIO.summary: S3-compatible object storage — MinIO (local dev) / E2E Object Storage (prod). Accessed via the @aws-sdk/client-s3 client (forcePathStyle) in FilesService; the bucket is shared and must be provisioned ahead of time (not auto-created).

queue:email-ingest-thread.summary: Per-thread ingestion job enqueued by the live poller; ingest-thread.worker.ts parses the thread, dedupes by VERP token, creates/updates Ticket+Message rows.

queue:email-send-reply.summary: Enqueued when an agent sends a reply via portal+email; send-reply.worker.ts sends via Nodemailer/Graph with VERP reply-to threading.

queue:email-send-confirmation.summary: Sends the ticket-received confirmation email via send-confirmation.worker.ts.

queue:ai-analyze-message.summary: Sentiment analysis for each inbound message.

queue:ai-classify-ticket.summary: Topic classification on ticket creation/update; also computes the AI CSAT estimate.

queue:ai-request-csat.summary: Sends the CSAT rating-request email when a ticket is resolved, creating a TicketRating row.

queue:bot-respond-to-ticket.summary: Enqueued on new ticket creation; Athena retrieves KB context and replies or escalates to the on-shift agent.

queue:kb-crawl-and-index.summary: Crawls AppConfig.kbRootUrl and indexes discovered pages.

queue:kb-scan.summary: Phase 1 of (re)index: scans pages, estimates embedding cost (kbScanPagesSeen/kbScanCostUsd).

queue:kb-embed.summary: Phase 2: embeds chunks into pgvector after scan confirmation (kbEmbedChunksDone/kbEmbedChunksTotal).

queue:kb-index-page.summary: Per-page indexing job that chunks and embeds a single page's content.

feature:email.summary: Two-way Gmail REST + Microsoft Graph polling sync; inbound mail becomes tickets/messages, outbound replies sent via Nodemailer/Graph. Confirmation email quotes customer's own portal description (delta watermark). Portal replies trigger a 'Received your response' ack to the customer (sendPortalReplyAck). Agent/bot reply emails are quote-free — thread lives in the email client's native chain. Reply + portal-ack sends carry an HTML body (bodyHtml ?? body) with a tag-stripped plain-text fallback (htmlToPlainText) so formatting renders instead of arriving as literal tags (R258). sendAgentReply also attaches the message's non-link file attachments — fetched from MinIO via FilesService.getAttachmentBuffer — so agent-uploaded files reach the customer's email, not just the portal (R260).

feature:tickets.summary: Core ticketing aggregate — status lifecycle (NEW/OPEN/IN_PROGRESS/WAITING/RESOLVED/CLOSED/DISMISSED), agent assignment, convert/discard for portal-submitted non-tickets, GitHub linking, configurable field1/field2.

feature:messages.summary: Per-ticket message thread: customer replies, agent replies (sent via portal and/or email with optional sticky CC), and internal-only notes; CC participants tracked in TicketParticipant; per-message sentiment from AI analysis.

feature:github.summary: Links tickets to GitHub issues (create/link/unlink). A webhook on issue label/state changes syncs the issue's live labels+state onto the ticket, appends a GithubIssueEvent timeline entry, raises the Ticket.githubUpdatePending attention flag (without touching TicketStatus), and posts a GITHUB_ISSUE_UPDATED notification. No label configuration.

feature:notifications.summary: In-app agent notifications (GitHub issue-updated, churn-risk detected), with per-agent read tracking and unread counts; delivered in real time via SSE and surfaced in the dashboard bell panel.

feature:analytics.summary: Operations dashboard (ticket volume/SLA/topics) and customer-intelligence dashboard (churn-risk/advocacy signals, CSAT), plus the customer-facing ratings form. All metrics and aggregates are scoped to isTicket=true — NEW conversations and DISMISSED rows are excluded from both dashboards.

feature:ai.summary: Background Gemini-powered analysis of tickets/messages: sentiment scoring, topic classification, CSAT request generation, and customer-signal (churn-risk/advocacy) extraction; usage/cost tracked per call. All analysis is gated on isTicket=true — NEW conversations and DISMISSED rows receive no AI processing. On convert(), prior unanalyzed customer messages are retroactively backfilled for sentiment. Both analysis workers classify failures via isTransientGeminiError (common/ai/transient-error.ts): transient Gemini errors (503/429, network) are rethrown so pg-boss retries with backoff, terminal errors (4xx, zod/JSON parse) are swallowed so a deterministic failure doesn't burn the retry budget (R266).

feature:files.summary: Upload/storage for ticket & message attachments in S3-compatible object storage (MinIO local / E2E prod) via the @aws-sdk/client-s3 client. Config (endpoint/SSL/region) is env-driven (S3_* vars). Downloads are signed fresh on click (short-lived URL), not persisted.

feature:settings.summary: Single-row instance-wide configuration: branding (logo, colors, portal hero copy), email/OAuth credentials, GitHub webhook config, bot/KB settings, feature flags, SLA, and configurable field1/field2 ticket fields.

feature:auth.summary: Customer auth (email/password, Google OAuth, guest sessions, SSO handoff, email verification, password reset via magic tokens) and separate agent auth (password/Google) for the dashboard.

feature:queue.summary: pg-boss-backed background job queues for email ingestion/sending, AI analysis, and bot/KB indexing — durable Postgres-backed queues, no separate broker.

feature:bot.summary: AI first-responder that answers new tickets from a crawled knowledge base using hybrid RAG (pgvector + Postgres FTS, RRF fusion), escalating to the on-shift agent when it can't answer confidently. appendSource() appends a single 'Learn more' KB deep-link, label drawn from the chunk's headingPath and sanitized via stripInlineMarkdown so Turndown anchor-linked headings don't leak markdown / double the slug (R259); the chunker applies the same strip at index time. On a Gemini failure, respondTo classifies via isTransientGeminiError (common/ai/transient-error.ts): a transient error (503/429/network) with retries left is rethrown so pg-boss retries the job with backoff (retryLimit 3); it escalates to a human only on a terminal error or the final attempt (isFinalAttempt, threaded from job.retrycount/retrylimit) — previously a single 503 escalated immediately and the queue's retryLimit never fired (R266).

module:TagsModule.summary: NestJS module — apps/api/src/modules/tags (tag CRUD: name-unique, fixed palette, agent-only)

module:CannedResponsesModule.summary: NestJS module — apps/api/src/modules/canned-responses (canned response CRUD; slash-command insertion in composer)

module:TmrDataModule.summary: NestJS module — apps/api/src/modules/tmr-data (TMR product metadata sync via back-office API; cached on User row)

feature:tmr-data.summary: TMR product metadata — fetches account/plan/team data from internal tmr_data_service and caches on User; shown in bridge CustomerProfilePanel + ticket sidebar. Server-to-server auth: Authorization: Bearer <TMR_DATA_SERVICE_API_KEY> + x-auth-mode: service marker, validated by the back-office's constant-time API-key branch (separate from its frontend JWT flow).

feature:logging.summary: App-wide structured logging via winston (WinstonLogger, set as the Nest app logger in main.ts): daily-rotating JSON file logs (apps/api/logs/app-YYYY-MM-DD.log, shape {ts,level,context,msg}) plus GCP Cloud Logging. No console transport; 256KB payload truncation guard.

ext:GoogleCloudLogging.summary: GCP Cloud Logging — managed log aggregation/Error Reporting sink.

route:GET /api/v1/files/:id/sign.summary: GET /api/v1/files/:id/sign → FilesController.sign (mint fresh short-lived download URL on click, auth+ownership checked)

entity:AppConfig.notes: Single-row instance config — ~80 columns spanning branding, email OAuth, GitHub webhook, bot/KB, feature flags. See _generated/erd.md for the full list.

queue:email-ingest-thread.name: email:ingest-thread
queue:email-ingest-thread.notes: Per-thread ingestion job enqueued by the live poller; ingest-thread.worker.ts parses the thread, dedupes by VERP token, creates/updates Ticket+Message rows.

queue:email-send-reply.name: email:send-reply
queue:email-send-reply.notes: Enqueued when an agent sends a reply via portal+email; send-reply.worker.ts sends via Nodemailer/Graph with VERP reply-to threading.

queue:email-send-confirmation.name: email:send-confirmation
queue:email-send-confirmation.notes: Sends the ticket-received confirmation email via send-confirmation.worker.ts.

queue:ai-analyze-message.name: ai:analyze-message
queue:ai-analyze-message.notes: Sentiment analysis for each inbound message.

queue:ai-classify-ticket.name: ai:classify-ticket
queue:ai-classify-ticket.notes: Topic classification on ticket creation/update; also computes the AI CSAT estimate.

queue:ai-request-csat.name: ai:request-csat
queue:ai-request-csat.notes: Sends the CSAT rating-request email when a ticket is resolved, creating a TicketRating row.

queue:bot-respond-to-ticket.name: bot:respond-to-ticket
queue:bot-respond-to-ticket.notes: Enqueued on new ticket creation; Athena retrieves KB context and replies or escalates to the on-shift agent.

queue:kb-crawl-and-index.name: kb:crawl-and-index
queue:kb-crawl-and-index.notes: Crawls AppConfig.kbRootUrl and indexes discovered pages.

queue:kb-scan.name: kb:scan
queue:kb-scan.notes: Phase 1 of (re)index: scans pages, estimates embedding cost (kbScanPagesSeen/kbScanCostUsd).

queue:kb-embed.name: kb:embed
queue:kb-embed.notes: Phase 2: embeds chunks into pgvector after scan confirmation (kbEmbedChunksDone/kbEmbedChunksTotal).

queue:kb-index-page.name: kb:index-page
queue:kb-index-page.notes: Per-page indexing job that chunks and embeds a single page's content.

feature:email.title: Email Sync
feature:email.summary: Two-way Gmail REST + Microsoft Graph polling sync; inbound mail becomes tickets/messages, outbound replies sent via Nodemailer/Graph. Confirmation email quotes customer's own portal description (delta watermark). Portal replies trigger a 'Received your response' ack to the customer (sendPortalReplyAck). Agent/bot reply emails are quote-free — thread lives in the email client's native chain. Reply + portal-ack sends carry an HTML body (bodyHtml ?? body) with a tag-stripped plain-text fallback (htmlToPlainText) so formatting renders instead of arriving as literal tags (R258). sendAgentReply also attaches the message's non-link file attachments — fetched from MinIO via FilesService.getAttachmentBuffer — so agent-uploaded files reach the customer's email, not just the portal (R260).
feature:email.why: Polling (not SMTP/IMAP IDLE) because Gmail/Graph REST APIs give reliable history/delta cursors and OAuth-scoped access without running a mailbox-watching daemon; VERP reply-to addresses encode the ticket/thread id so inbound replies are matched without parsing subject lines.
gaps: No documented backoff/retry tuning for Graph 429 rate limits
Backfill/archive progress lives on the single AppConfig row, so only one mailbox syncs at a time (single-tenant assumption)
Delta-quoting is text-only (Message.body); an HTML-aware quoted-history block (bodyHtml) is a documented follow-up.

feature:tickets.title: Tickets
feature:tickets.summary: Core ticketing aggregate — status lifecycle (NEW/OPEN/IN_PROGRESS/WAITING/RESOLVED/CLOSED/DISMISSED), agent assignment, convert/discard for portal-submitted non-tickets, GitHub linking, configurable field1/field2.
feature:tickets.why: isTicket + status (NEW vs DISMISSED) distinguishes real support requests from other inbound mail/portal submissions; convert/discard let agents reclassify after triage.
gaps: A triage→status redesign (NEW/DISMISSED inbox split with dedicated tabs) was planned separately — see STATE.md decisions

feature:messages.title: Messages & Notes
feature:messages.summary: Per-ticket message thread: customer replies, agent replies (sent via portal and/or email with optional sticky CC), and internal-only notes; CC participants tracked in TicketParticipant; per-message sentiment from AI analysis.
feature:messages.why: MessageType (REPLY/INTERNAL_NOTE/SYSTEM_EVENT) and MessageSentVia (PORTAL/EMAIL/PORTAL_AND_EMAIL) let one model represent customer-visible replies, internal notes, and system events, and record how an agent reply was delivered.

feature:github.title: GitHub Integration
feature:github.summary: Links tickets to GitHub issues (create/link/unlink). A webhook on issue label/state changes syncs the issue's live labels+state onto the ticket, appends a GithubIssueEvent timeline entry, raises the Ticket.githubUpdatePending attention flag (without touching TicketStatus), and posts a GITHUB_ISSUE_UPDATED notification. No label configuration.
feature:github.why: A single GithubConfig row (single-tenant model) stores the connected account's access token; webhooks are verified against AppConfig.githubWebhookSecret. A label/state change is treated as a human-in-the-loop signal (attention flag), never an automated TicketStatus change — analytics/bot/kanban bucket on TicketStatus.
gaps: Label colours are sourced from the webhook payload and can drift if a label is recoloured with no subsequent issue event
Only one linked issue per ticket

feature:notifications.title: Notifications
feature:notifications.summary: In-app agent notifications (GitHub issue-updated, churn-risk detected), with per-agent read tracking and unread counts; delivered in real time via SSE and surfaced in the dashboard bell panel.
feature:notifications.why: NotificationRead is a per-agent join table so unread counts/read-state are agent-specific even though Notification rows are global (single-tenant, shared inbox).

feature:analytics.title: Analytics
feature:analytics.summary: Operations dashboard (ticket volume/SLA/topics) and customer-intelligence dashboard (churn-risk/advocacy signals, CSAT), plus the customer-facing ratings form. All metrics and aggregates are scoped to isTicket=true — NEW conversations and DISMISSED rows are excluded from both dashboards.
feature:analytics.why: TicketRating.ratingToken is a single-use unauthenticated token emailed to the customer so the CSAT form works without a portal login.

feature:ai.title: AI Ticket Analysis
feature:ai.summary: Background Gemini-powered analysis of tickets/messages: sentiment scoring, topic classification, CSAT request generation, and customer-signal (churn-risk/advocacy) extraction; usage/cost tracked per call. All analysis is gated on isTicket=true — NEW conversations and DISMISSED rows receive no AI processing. On convert(), prior unanalyzed customer messages are retroactively backfilled for sentiment. Both analysis workers classify failures via isTransientGeminiError (common/ai/transient-error.ts): transient Gemini errors (503/429, network) are rethrown so pg-boss retries with backoff, terminal errors (4xx, zod/JSON parse) are swallowed so a deterministic failure doesn't burn the retry budget (R266).
feature:ai.why: Every Gemini call is recorded as an AiUsage row (model, tokens, cost, status) for the AI usage settings page and cost visibility — single-tenant so usage is instance-wide, not per-org.
gaps: AiOperation also includes ATHENA_EMBED/ATHENA_GENERATE/KB_CONTEXTUAL_SUMMARY which belong to feature:bot's usage — AiUsage is shared across both features

feature:files.title: Files & Attachments
feature:files.summary: Upload/storage for ticket & message attachments in S3-compatible object storage (MinIO local / E2E prod) via the @aws-sdk/client-s3 client. Config (endpoint/SSL/region) is env-driven (S3_* vars). Downloads are signed fresh on click (short-lived URL), not persisted.
feature:files.why: Download URLs are minted per-click via GET /files/:id/sign (durable objectKey + short TTL) so links never go stale and a leaked URL expires fast; risky types are force-downloaded. Attachment.isLink/linkUrl represents external links instead of stored blobs.
gaps: Upload extension block is minimal (.exe only).

feature:settings.title: Settings / AppConfig
feature:settings.summary: Single-row instance-wide configuration: branding (logo, colors, portal hero copy), email/OAuth credentials, GitHub webhook config, bot/KB settings, feature flags, SLA, and configurable field1/field2 ticket fields.
feature:settings.why: Single-tenant: no Org model, so every cross-feature setting (branding, integration credentials, feature flags) lives on one AppConfig row instead of per-tenant config.
gaps: AppConfig has grown to ~80 columns spanning email/GitHub/bot/KB/branding/feature-flags — see _generated/erd.md for the full field list

feature:auth.title: Auth
feature:auth.summary: Customer auth (email/password, Google OAuth, guest sessions, SSO handoff, email verification, password reset via magic tokens) and separate agent auth (password/Google/accept-invite) for the dashboard.
feature:auth.why: MagicToken (EMAIL_VERIFICATION / PASSWORD_RESET) is a shared single-use-token table for both flows; guest sessions create an isGuest User so unauthenticated portal visitors can still open tickets. SSO handoff uses HMAC shared secret (HS256) matching Intercom's identity-verification model — simple, single-tenant, first-party-only. Agent invite accept flow: invite() sends email with token, accept-invite endpoint sets password + clears token.
gaps: SsoUsedToken cleanup cron not yet implemented — expired rows accumulate indefinitely
RS256 mode (for untrusted third-party host apps) not yet supported

feature:queue.title: Job Queue
feature:queue.summary: pg-boss-backed background job queues for email ingestion/sending, AI analysis, and bot/KB indexing — durable Postgres-backed queues, no separate broker.
feature:queue.why: pg-boss was chosen over Redis/BullMQ to avoid an extra infra dependency — jobs are durable rows in the same Postgres database.

feature:bot.title: Athena Bot (First Responder + KB)
feature:bot.summary: AI first-responder that answers new tickets from a crawled knowledge base using hybrid RAG (pgvector + Postgres FTS, RRF fusion), escalating to the on-shift agent when it can't answer confidently. appendSource() appends a single 'Learn more' KB deep-link, label drawn from the chunk's headingPath and sanitized via stripInlineMarkdown so Turndown anchor-linked headings don't leak markdown / double the slug (R259); the chunker applies the same strip at index time. On a Gemini failure, respondTo classifies via isTransientGeminiError (common/ai/transient-error.ts): a transient error (503/429/network) with retries left is rethrown so pg-boss retries the job with backoff (retryLimit 3); it escalates to a human only on a terminal error or the final attempt (isFinalAttempt, threaded from job.retrycount/retrylimit) — previously a single 503 escalated immediately and the queue's retryLimit never fired (R266).
feature:bot.why: Hybrid retrieval (vector similarity + keyword FTS, fused with RRF) covers both semantic and exact-term queries; BotInteraction records retrieval scores/confidence/cost per attempt for tuning. Shift-based escalation picks the on-shift agent (primaryAgentId by dayOfWeek/time window) when the bot doesn't answer.
gaps: KB scan/embed is a two-phase confirm flow (scan estimates cost, embed commits) — KbPhase enum tracks IDLE/SCANNING/AWAITING_CONFIRM/EMBEDDING/DONE/FAILED/CANCELLED on AppConfig; if 0 pages are successfully scanned (e.g. unreachable URL), kbPhase is set to FAILED with kbError message instead of AWAITING_CONFIRM

feature:tmr-data.name: tmr-data
feature:tmr-data.summary: TMR product metadata — fetches account/plan/team data from internal tmr_data_service and caches on User; shown in bridge CustomerProfilePanel + ticket sidebar. Server-to-server auth: Authorization: Bearer <TMR_DATA_SERVICE_API_KEY> + x-auth-mode: service marker, validated by the back-office's constant-time API-key branch (separate from its frontend JWT flow).

feature:logging.title: Logging & Observability
feature:logging.summary: App-wide structured logging via winston (WinstonLogger, set as the Nest app logger in main.ts): daily-rotating JSON file logs (apps/api/logs/app-YYYY-MM-DD.log, shape {ts,level,context,msg}) plus GCP Cloud Logging. No console transport; 256KB payload truncation guard.
feature:logging.why: Mirrors tmr_data_service/src/configs/logger.ts — centralizes logs in GCP for production while preserving the local jq-parseable file format. GCP writes are non-fatal (defaultCallback) so a missing/invalid key file logs to stderr and never crashes the app.

beats.say: Customer replies to (or starts) a support thread via Gmail/Graph
Live poller (live-poller.service.ts) detects new history via the Gmail/Graph delta cursor
Poller enqueues an ingest-thread job for the new message
ingest-thread.worker parses the thread and matches the VERP reply-to token to find/create the ticket
Ticket created or updated (status NEW); Message row inserted
New-ticket hook enqueues Athena to attempt a first response
Topic classification + sentiment analysis queued

journey:send-reply.title: Agent reply goes out over email

beats.say: Agent opens the ticket in the Dashboard
Agent writes a reply, choosing Portal + Email delivery
Message row created (type REPLY, sentVia PORTAL_AND_EMAIL)
send-reply job enqueued
send-reply.worker sends via Nodemailer/Graph with VERP reply-to for threading
Customer receives the reply and can respond to continue the thread

journey:bot-first-response.title: Athena answers or escalates a new ticket

beats.say: New ticket created (status NEW)
respond-to-ticket job enqueued
Athena retrieves KB chunks via hybrid search (pgvector + Postgres FTS, RRF fusion)
Top-ranked chunks + citations gathered as context
Gemini generates an answer grounded in the retrieved chunks
Interaction logged: confidence, chunks used, cost, tokens
If confidence is too low, the on-shift agent (per Shift schedule) is assigned and notified instead
Athena's reply (or escalation note) is posted to the ticket
Agent marks the ticket RESOLVED
CSAT request job enqueued
TicketRating row created with a single-use ratingToken
Rating-request email sent to the customer with the rating link
Customer opens the unauthenticated rating form
Customer submits a 1-5 rating + optional comment
Gemini also computes an AI CSAT estimate (aiRating/aiReasoning) for comparison
Ratings feed the customer-intelligence dashboard
Customer signs up with email/password (or POST /auth/google for OAuth)
User row created (source PORTAL, isVerified=false)
EMAIL_VERIFICATION magic token issued and emailed
Customer clicks the verification link; token validated, isVerified set true
Subsequent sign-ins use email/password (or Google) to receive a JWT
Authenticated customer can now view their tickets in the Portal

journey:portal-support-flow.title: Portal ticket: open → confirm → bot → reply → resolve

beats.say: Customer opens a ticket in the portal (source=PORTAL); Ticket + first Message row created
Ticket activated (isTicket=true, status=OPEN); confirmation job enqueued
SendConfirmationWorker sends confirmation email (thread root) quoting the customer's description
Bot job enqueued; Athena retrieves KB chunks (hybrid RAG) and generates an answer
Bot posts reply message; if confident it answers, otherwise escalates to a human
SendReplyWorker emails the bot reply to the customer — no quoted history block
Customer replies from the portal; sendPortalReplyAck sends 'Received your response' email To the customer, threaded
Agent replies from Bridge; sendAgentReply emails the customer — no quoted history block

journey:email-support-flow.title: Direct email: inbound → ticket → ongoing thread replies

beats.say: Customer emails the support address directly
Live poller detects new history via the Gmail/Graph delta cursor
Poller enqueues ingest-thread job; IngestThreadWorker processes it
Thread ingested: Ticket created (source=EMAIL, isTicket=false) + Message row (sentVia=EMAIL, customerEmailedAt pre-marked)
AI classifies the ticket (sentiment, topic, CSAT signals)
Agent converts conversation to ticket in Bridge (isTicket=true); confirmation + bot enqueued
Customer replies in email thread → ingested, matched to ticket by externalThreadId, deduped by RFC Message-ID — no outbound ack (already in the thread)
Agent replies from Bridge → sendAgentReply delivers email to customer (no quoted history)
